library_preparation_preflight.summary: Spring Boot Liquibase auto-configuration only becomes meaningful when Liquibase can run against a JDBC DataSource; the resolved artifact brings Spring Boot JDBC and liquibase-core but no database driver.

deterministic_setup.reason: Spring Boot Liquibase normally runs database migrations against a DataSource. H2 provides an in-memory JDBC database so generated tests can exercise LiquibaseAutoConfiguration and actual changelog execution without Docker.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Use a small in-memory H2 setup and test changelog. Either place a changelog at the default `classpath:/db/changelog/db.changelog-master.yaml` or set `spring.liquibase.change-log` to a test resource. Configure a deterministic test datasource such as `spring.datasource.url=jdbc:h2:mem:liquibase-test;DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1`, `spring.datasource.username=sa`, and consider `spring.liquibase.analytics-enabled=false` to keep tests hermetic.
risks: The artifact also contains optional actuator, Docker Compose, and Testcontainers integration classes; broad coverage of those optional paths may need additional optional Spring Boot/Testcontainers dependencies, but they are not required for initial meaningful Liquibase auto-configuration coverage.
Local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative for the exact H2 version and any native-image reachability gaps.
